text: ```json { "type": "TEXT", "title": "Exploring Essential HTML Tags", "lessonText": "Dive into the core HTML tags that form the foundation of every webpage.", "data": { "content": "Essential HTML tags are the building blocks of web content, defining structure and meaning. * **``**: The root element that encapsulates all other HTML elements; as seen in 'Building a Basic HTML Structure'. * **``**: Contains metadata about the HTML document, such as the title, character set, and linked stylesheets. * **``**: Specifies a title for the HTML page (which is shown in the browser's title bar or tab). * **`<body>`**: Contains the visible page content, including text, images, and other elements. * **`<header>`**: Represents introductory content, typically containing a heading, logo, or navigation. * **`<nav>`**: Defines a set of navigation links. * **`<main>`**: Specifies the main content of a document. * **`<article>`**: Represents a self-contained composition in a document, page, application, or site. * **`<section>`**: Defines a thematic grouping of content, typically with a heading. * **`<aside>`**: Defines content aside from the page content (like a sidebar). * **`<footer>`**: Defines a footer for a document or section. * **`<p>`**: Represents a paragraph of text. * **`<h1>` to `<h6>`**: Define headings of different levels. * **`<a>`**: Creates a hyperlink to other web pages or resources. * **`<img>`**: Embeds an image in the HTML document. * **`<div>`**: A generic container for flow content, which does not inherently represent anything. It can be used to group elements for styling purposes. * **`<span>`**: An inline container used to mark up a part of a text, or a part of a document. It has no required attributes, but style and class attributes are common. Understanding these tags is crucial for creating well-structured and semantic HTML, as emphasized in 'HTML Foundations: Structure and Semantics'." } } ```
